title: Eriocaulon infirmum
intro: Eriocaulon infirmum, also known as infirmum pipewort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as arthritis and diabetes. The bioactive constituents of Eriocaulon infirmum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with infirmum pipewort are tea, tinctures, and capsules, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Eriocaulon infirmum is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly known as pipewort. It belongs to the genus Eriocaulon, a group of plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. The Eriocaulaceae family is an order of monocotyledonous flowering plants that are primarily aquatic or semi-aquatic. Eriocaulon infirmum is often found in wetland areas, where it grows in dense clumps. The classification of Eriocaulon infirmum is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Monocots, order Eriocaulales, family Eriocaulaceae, genus Eriocaulon, species E. infirmum.
